answer:
The interval of convergence for a power series must be an interval centered at the expansion point, so it cannot be a union of disjoint intervals like [2,2] ∪ [3,3] in (III).  
It also cannot be half-infinite on the left like (−∞, 0] in (II), because convergence intervals are symmetric around the center unless endpoints are tested.  
Thus only (I) [−1, 3) and (IV) (−∞,∞) are valid forms, making the correct choice E.

\boxed{E}
